Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Investment Canada Act
Identification.ShortTitle: National Security Review of Investments Modernization Act
Identification.RunningHead: National Security Review of Investments Modernization Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Investment Canada Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: require notice of certain investments to be given prior to their implementation;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of Industry, after consultation with the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, to impose interim conditions in respect of investments in order to prevent injury to national security that could arise during the review;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: require, in certain cases, the Minister of Industry to make an order for the further review of investments under Part IV.1;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: allow written undertakings to be submitted to the Minister of Industry to address risks of injury to national security and allow that Minister, with the concurrence of the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, to complete consideration of an investment because of the undertakings;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: introduce rules for the protection of information in the course of judicial review proceedings in relation to decisions and orders under Part IV.1;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of Industry to disclose information that is otherwise privileged under the Act to foreign states for the purposes of foreign investment reviews;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: establish a penalty not exceeding the greater of $500,000 and any prescribed amount, for failure to give notice of, or file applications with respect to, certain investments; and

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: increase the penalty for other contraventions of the Act or the regulations to the greater of $25,000 and any prescribed amount for each day of the contravention.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the National Security Review of Investments Modernization Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 11 of the Investment Canada Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an investment to acquire, in whole or in part, an entity carrying on all or any part of its operations in Canada and that has a place of operations in Canada, an individual or individuals in Canada who are employed or self-employed in connection with the entity’s operations or assets in Canada used in carrying on the entity’s operations, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity carries on a prescribed business activity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-Canadian could, as a result of the investment, have access to, or direct the use of, material non-public technical information or material assets, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-Canadian would have, as a result of the investment,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the power to appoint or nominate any person who has the capacity to direct the business and affairs of the entity, such as a member of the board of directors or of senior management, a trustee of the entity or, in the case of a limited partnership, a general partner, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: prescribed special rights with respect to the entity.

Subsection.Text: Section 11 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 11(1) and is amended by adding the following:
The Governor in Council may make regulations defining “material assets” and “material non-public technical information” for the purposes of subparagraph (1)(c)(ii).

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A non-Canadian making an investment shall, in the prescribed manner, give notice of the investment that includes prescribed information to the Director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an investment referred to in paragraph 11(1)(b) in respect of a Canadian business that carries on a prescribed business activity or an investment referred to in paragraph 11(1)(c), no later than the prescribed time prior to the implementation of the investment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other investment, within the prescribed period.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections 25.2(2) and 25.3(3), a non-Canadian who gives notice of an investment under paragraph (1)(a) shall not implement the investment unless the periods referred to in subsections 25.2(1) and 25.3(1) have expired.
Subparagraphs 13(1)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the investment is not reviewable under Part IV, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: unless the Director sends the non-Canadian a notice for review under section 15 within 45 days after the certified date referred to in paragraph (a) or within the prescribed period, the investment is not reviewable under Part IV.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 13(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a notice given under section 12 is incomplete, the Director shall, within the prescribed period, send a notice to the non-Canadian, specifying the information required to complete the notice under section 12 and requesting that the information be provided to the Director in order to complete that notice.
The portion of subsection 13(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An investment in respect of which a receipt is sent under subsection (1) is not reviewable under Part IV if
Paragraph 13(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in a case where the receipt contains the advice referred to in subparagraph (1)(b)(ii), no notice for review is sent to the non-Canadian pursuant to section 15 within 45 days after the certified date referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or within the prescribed period.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(v) of the definition WTO investor in subsection 14.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: of which at least two-thirds of the members of its board of directors, or of which at least two-thirds of its general partners, as the case may be, are any combination of Canadians and WTO investors,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (e)(iv) of the definition WTO investor in subsection 14.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: of which at least two-thirds of its trustees are any combination of Canadians and WTO investors, or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(v) of the definition trade agreement investor in subsection 14.11(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: at least two-thirds of the members of its board of directors, or at least two-thirds of its general partners, as the case may be, are any combination of Canadians and trade agreement investors;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (e)(iv) of the definition trade agreement investor in subsection 14.11(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: at least two-thirds of its trustees are any combination of Canadians and trade agreement investors; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 15 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: An investment referred to in paragraph 11(1)(a) or (b) that is subject to notification under Part III and that would not otherwise be reviewable under this Part is reviewable under this Part if

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 15(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within 45 days after the certified date referred to in paragraph 13(1)(a)

Subsection.Text: Section 15 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 15(1) and is amended by adding the following:
Despite the limits set out in subsections 14(3), 14.1(1) and (1.1) and 14.11(1) and (2), an investment is reviewable under this Part if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the non-Canadian making the investment is a state-owned enterprise or is controlled by a state-owned enterprise unless the non-Canadian is a trade agreement investor;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Governor in Council, on the recommendation of the Minister, is of the opinion that a review of the investment is in the public interest;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Governor in Council issues an order for the review within 45 days after the certified date referred to in paragraph 13(1)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Director sends the non-Canadian making the investment a notice for review.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 17(2)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an investment made through an acquisition referred to in subparagraph 28(1)(d)(ii) or an investment with respect to which a notice referred to in paragraph 16(2)(a) has been sent, within the prescribed period; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an investment reviewable pursuant to section 15, forthwith on receipt of a notice for review referred to in subparagraph 15(1)(b)(ii) or paragraph 15(2)(d).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 20(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the effect of the investment on productivity, industrial efficiency, technological development, product innovation and product variety in Canada, including, for greater certainty, the effect of the investment on any rights relating to intellectual property whose development has been funded, in whole or in part, by the Government of Canada;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 20(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the compatibility of the investment with national industrial, economic and cultural policies, taking into consideration industrial, economic and cultural policy objectives enunciated by the government or legislature of any province likely to be significantly affected by the investment, and including, for greater certainty, the effect of the investment on the use and protection of personal information about Canadians; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 21(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Sous réserve des paragraphes (4) et (5), si, avant l’expiration du délai de quarante-cinq jours visé au paragraphe (1), l’avis prévu au paragraphe 25.2(1) est envoyé relativement à l’investissement et si l’arrêté visé au paragraphe 25.3(1) est pris relativement à celui-ci, le délai pendant lequel le ministre peut envoyer l’avis prévu au paragraphe (1) expire trente jours après l’expiration du délai réglementaire ou du délai supplémentaire visés aux paragraphes 25.3(6) ou (7), selon le cas, ou à l’expiration de tout délai supplémentaire sur lequel celui-ci et le demandeur s’entendent.
Subsection 21(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, before the end of the 45-day period referred to in subsection (1), a notice is sent under subsection 25.2(1) in respect of the investment and if, in respect of the investment, an order is made under subsection 25.3(1) and a notice under paragraph 25.3(6)(b) or (c) is sent, the period during which the Minister may send the notice referred to in subsection (1) expires 30 days after the day on which the notice under that paragraph was sent or at the end of any further period that the Minister and the applicant agree on.
The portion of subsection 21(5) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Si, avant l’expiration du délai de quarante-cinq jours visé au paragraphe (1), l’avis prévu au paragraphe 25.2(1) est envoyé relativement à l’investissement et si l’arrêté visé au paragraphe 25.3(1) est pris relativement à celui-ci et que le ministre renvoie la question au gouverneur en conseil en application de l’alinéa 25.3(6)a) ou du paragraphe 25.3(7), le délai pendant lequel le ministre peut envoyer l’avis prévu au paragraphe (1) expire trente jours après celle des dates ci-après qui est antérieure à l’autre ou à l’expiration de tout délai supplémentaire sur lequel celui-ci et le demandeur s’entendent :
Subsection 21(6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Sous réserve des paragraphes (7) et (8), si, avant l’expiration du délai de quarante-cinq jours visé au paragraphe (1), l’arrêté visé au paragraphe 25.3(1) est pris relativement à l’investissement, le délai pendant lequel le ministre peut envoyer l’avis prévu au paragraphe (1) expire trente jours après l’expiration du délai réglementaire ou du délai supplémentaire visés aux paragraphes 25.3(6) ou (7), selon le cas, ou à l’expiration de tout délai supplémentaire sur lequel celui-ci et le demandeur s’entendent.
Subsection 21(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, before the end of the 45-day period referred to in subsection (1), an order is made under subsection 25.3(1) in respect of the investment and if, in respect of the investment, a notice is sent under paragraph 25.3(6)(b) or (c), the period during which the Minister may send the notice referred to in subsection (1) expires 30 days after the day on which the notice under that paragraph was sent or at the end of any further period that the Minister and the applicant agree on.
The portion of subsection 21(8) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Si, avant l’expiration du délai de quarante-cinq jours visé au paragraphe (1), l’arrêté visé au paragraphe 25.3(1) est pris relativement à l’investissement et si le ministre renvoie la question au gouverneur en conseil en application de l’alinéa 25.3(6)a) ou du paragraphe 25.3(7), le délai pendant lequel le ministre peut envoyer l’avis prévu au paragraphe (1) expire trente jours après celle des dates ci-après qui est antérieure à l’autre ou à l’expiration de tout délai supplémentaire sur lequel celui-ci et le demandeur s’entendent :
Subparagraph (d)(v) of the definition CUSMA investor in subsection 24(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: of which at least two-thirds of the members of its board of directors, or of which at least two-thirds of its general partners, as the case may be, are any combination of Canadians and CUSMA investors;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (e)(iv) of the definition CUSMA investor in subsection 24(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: of which at least two-thirds of its trustees are any combination of Canadians and CUSMA investors; or

Section.Text: The heading of Part IV.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Review of Investments — Injurious to National Security

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 25.1(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to acquire control of a Canadian business in any manner described in section 28;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: if the non-Canadian is a state-owned enterprise, to acquire any of the assets of a Canadian business; or

Subsection.Text: Section 25.1 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 25.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:
For greater certainty, paragraph (1)(c) includes an investment to acquire, in whole or in part, the assets of an entity referred to in that paragraph.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 25.1:
The review of an investment under this Part begins on the day on which it first comes to the attention of the Minister.

The Minister may require the non-Canadian or other person or entity from which the Canadian business or the entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c) is being acquired to provide to the Minister, within the time and in the manner specified by the Minister, any prescribed information or any other information that the Minister considers necessary for the purposes of the review.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 25.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that an investment by a non-Canadian could be injurious to national security, the Minister may, within the prescribed period, send to the non-Canadian a notice that an order for the further review of the investment may be made under subsection 25.3(1).

For the purposes of subsection (1), the fact that a non-Canadian has previously been convicted, within or outside Canada, for an offence involving an act of corruption constitutes, by itself, reasonable grounds.
Paragraphs 25.2(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice under subsection (4);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a notice under paragraph 25.3(6)(b) or (c); or

Subsection.Text: Subsections 25.2(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Minister, after the consultation referred to in subsection 25.3(1), does not consider that the investment could be injurious to national security, the Minister shall, within the prescribed period, send to the non-Canadian a notice indicating that consideration of the investment is complete and that no order will be made under that subsection.
Subsections 25.3(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, considers that an investment could be injurious to national security, the Minister shall make an order within the prescribed period for the further review of the investment.

The Minister shall, by order, impose interim conditions in respect of the investment that are applicable until no later than when consideration of the investment is complete, or amend such conditions, if the Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, is satisfied that it is necessary for the purpose of preventing injury to national security that could arise during that review, provided that the imposition of interim conditions does not introduce significant new risks of injury to national security. The Minister shall also delete a condition if the Minister, after such consultation, is satisfied that it is no longer necessary for that purpose.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Statutory Instruments Act does not apply
Subsection.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply in respect of an order made under this section.

The Minister shall, without delay after an order has been made under this section, send to the non-Canadian making the investment and to any person or entity from which the Canadian business or the entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c) is being acquired a notice indicating that the order has been made and advising them of their right to make representations and to submit undertakings to the Minister. A copy of the order is to accompany the notice.
Paragraph 25.3(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a notice under paragraph (6)(b) or (c);

Subsection.Text: Subsections 25.3(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

After receipt of the notice referred to in subsection (2), the non-Canadian or other person or entity may make representations and submit written undertakings, within the time and in the manner specified in the notice.
Subsections 25.3(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Minister shall, within the prescribed period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: after consultation with the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, refer the investment under review to the Governor in Council, together with a report of the Minister’s findings and recommendations on the review, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Minister is satisfied that the investment would be injurious to national security, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on the basis of the information available, the Minister is not able to determine whether the investment would be injurious to national security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after consultation with the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, send to the non-Canadian a notice indicating that consideration of the investment is complete, if the Minister is satisfied that the investment would not be injurious to national security; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: send to the non-Canadian a notice indicating that consideration of the investment is complete, if the Minister is satisfied, with the concurrence of the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, that the investment would not be injurious to national security because of the undertakings that are given to His Majesty in right of Canada.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister is unable to complete the consideration of an investment within the prescribed period referred to in subsection (6), the Minister shall, within that period, send a notice to that effect to the non-Canadian. The Minister then has until the end of the period prescribed for this subsection, or any further period that the Minister and the non-Canadian agree on, to take the applicable measures described in paragraph (6)(a), (b) or (c).
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 25.3:

Section.Text: After the Minister sends a notice referred to in paragraph 25.3(6)(c), the Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: accept new written undertakings if the Minister is satisfied, with the concurrence of the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, that the risks of injury to national security identified in the notice would continue to be addressed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: release the non-Canadian or other person or entity from any undertakings, if the Minister is satisfied, with the concurrence of the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, that they are no longer necessary to address those risks.

Section.Text: Paragraph 25.4(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the non-Canadian to make the investment on the terms and conditions contained in the order; or

Section.Text: Sections 25.5 and 25.6 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Non-Canadians or other persons or entities shall, within the time and in the manner specified by the Director, submit any information relating to the investment that is required by the Director in order to permit the Director to determine whether they are complying with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under section 25.3 or 25.4; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any written undertaking given to His Majesty in right of Canada and referred to in paragraph 25.3(6)(c) or 25.31(a).

Section.Text: Decisions and orders of the Governor in Council and of the Minister made under this Part are final and binding and, except for judicial review under the Federal Courts Act, are not subject to appeal or to review by any court.

Subsection.Text: The following provisions apply to the judicial review of a decision or order made under this Part:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time during a proceeding, the judge shall, on the request of the Minister, hear submissions on evidence or other information in the absence of the public and of the applicant and their counsel if, in the judge’s opinion, the disclosure of the evidence or other information could be injurious to international relations, national defence or national security or could endanger the safety of any person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the judge shall ensure the confidentiality of the evidence and other information provided by the Minister if, in the judge’s opinion, its disclosure would be injurious to international relations, national defence or national security or would endanger the safety of any person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: throughout the proceeding, the judge shall ensure that the applicant is provided with a summary of the evidence and other information available to the judge that enables the applicant to be reasonably informed of the Government of Canada’s case but that does not include anything that, in the judge’s opinion, would be injurious to international relations, national defence or national security or would endanger the safety of any person if disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the judge shall provide the applicant and the Minister with an opportunity to be heard;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the decision of the judge may be based on evidence or other information available to the judge even if a summary of that evidence or other information has not been provided to the applicant;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the judge determines that evidence or other information provided by the Minister is not relevant or if the Minister withdraws the evidence or other information, the decision of the judge shall not be based on that evidence or other information and the judge must return it to the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the judge shall ensure the confidentiality of all evidence and other information that the Minister withdraws.

Subsection.Text: In this section, judge means the Chief Justice of the Federal Court or a judge of that Court designated by the Chief Justice.

Section.Text: Section 25.7 applies to any appeal of a decision made under that section and to any further appeal, with any necessary modifications.

Within 30 days after sending a notice under paragraph 25.3(6)(c) or a copy of the order under subsection 25.4(2), the Minister shall notify the National Security and Intelligence Committee of Parliamentarians and the National Security and Intelligence Review Agency of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that a notice was sent under paragraph 25.3(6)(c) and of the identity of the non-Canadian and the Canadian business or entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that an order was made under subsection 25.4(1), of the identity of the non-Canadian and the Canadian business or entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c) that is the subject of the order, and of whether the order

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: directed the non-Canadian not to implement the investment that is the subject of the order,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: authorized the investment or contained terms and conditions, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: required the non-Canadian to divest themselves of control of the Canadian business, or of their investment in the entity, that is the subject of the order.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 26(1)(d)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity that is a corporation or limited partnership, the entity is not controlled in fact through the ownership of its voting interests and at least two-thirds of the members of its board of directors or, in the case of a limited partnership, at least two-thirds of its general partners, are Canadians.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 26(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (2.1) to (2.2), (2.31) and (2.32), if it can be established that a trust is not controlled in fact through the ownership of its voting interests, subsection (1) does not apply, and the trust is a Canadian-controlled entity if at least two-thirds of its trustees are Canadians.
Paragraph 26(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at least four-fifths of the members of its board of directors are Canadian citizens ordinarily resident in Canada,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 36(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Information that is privileged under subsection (1) may be communicated or disclosed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by the Minister to a prescribed investigative body, or an investigative body of a prescribed class, for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of Part IV.1 in the context of that body’s lawful investigations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by such an investigative body, for the purposes of its lawful investigations; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: by the Minister, on such terms and conditions that the Minister deems appropriate, to a government of a foreign state or an agency thereof that is responsible for the review of foreign investments, for the purpose of national security reviews of foreign investments.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 36(4)(e)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any notice sent under subsection 21(1) or (9), 22(2) or (4), 23(1) or (3), 25.2(1) or (4) or 25.3(2), paragraph 25.3(6)(b) or (c) or subsection 25.3(7), or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 36(4)(e.2)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: authorized the investment, including if it did so on terms and conditions, or

Subsection.Text: Section 36 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.1):

For greater certainty, when communicating or disclosing under paragraph (4)(e.2) the fact that an order was made under subsection 25.4(1), the Minister is not prohibited from communicating or disclosing the identity of the non-Canadian and of the Canadian business or entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c) that is the subject of the order.

Section.Text: Section 38.1 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 38.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: The report shall include information on the exercise of ministerial duties and powers under Part IV.1.
Paragraph 39(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has failed to give notice in accordance with section 12 or file an application in accordance with section 17,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has implemented an investment the implementation of which is prohibited by section 12, 16, 24, 25.2 or 25.3,

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 39(1)(d.1) and (d.2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: has failed to comply with a written undertaking given to His Majesty in right of Canada and referred to in paragraph 25.3(6)(c) or 25.31(a),

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: has failed to comply with an order made under section 25.3 or 25.4,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may send a demand to a person or entity requiring that they immediately, or within any period that may be specified in the demand, cease the contravention, remedy the default or show cause why there is no contravention of the Act if the Minister believes that the person or the entity has failed to comply with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a requirement to provide information under section 25.12 or 25.5;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a written undertaking given to His Majesty in right of Canada and referred to in paragraph 25.3(6)(c) or 25.31(a); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under section 25.3 or 25.4.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An application on behalf of the Minister may be made to a superior court for an order under subsection (2) or (2.1) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a non-Canadian fails to give notice in accordance with paragraph 12(1)(a) or to file, with respect to a Canadian business that carries on a prescribed business activity, an application in accordance with section 17; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a non-Canadian or any other person or entity fails to comply with a demand under section 39.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 40(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, at the conclusion of the hearing on an application referred to in subsection (1), the superior court decides that the non-Canadian has failed to give the notice or file the application referred to in paragraph (1)(a), or that the Minister was justified in sending a demand to the non-Canadian or other person or entity under section 39 and that the non-Canadian or other person or entity has failed to comply with the demand, the court may make any order or orders that, in its opinion, the circumstances require, including an order
Paragraphs 40(2)(c.1) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: directing the non-Canadian to comply with a written undertaking given to His Majesty in right of Canada and referred to in paragraph 25.3(6)(c) or 25.31(a);

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: directing the non-Canadian to comply with an order made under section 25.3;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: against the non-Canadian imposing a penalty not exceeding

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a failure to give notice in accordance with paragraph 12(1)(a) or to file, with respect to a Canadian business that carries on a prescribed business activity, an application in accordance with section 17, the greater of $500,000 and any prescribed amount, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of any other contravention of the provisions of this Act or the regulations, the greater of $25,000 and any prescribed amount for each day of the contravention;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, at the conclusion of the hearing on an application referred to in subsection (1), the superior court decides that the Minister was justified in sending a demand to a person or an entity under section 39 and that the person or entity has failed to comply with it, the court may make any order or orders that, in its opinion, the circumstances require, including an order against the person or entity imposing a penalty not exceeding the greater of $25,000 and any prescribed amount for each day of the contravention.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: former Act means the Investment Canada Act as it read immediately before the day on which section 15 comes into force. (ancienne loi)
new Act means the Investment Canada Act as it reads on the day on which section 15 comes into force. (nouvelle loi)

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), on or after the day on which section 15 comes into force, the review of any investment under Part IV.1 of the former Act in respect of which a measure has not been taken under subsection 25.3(6) of the former Act shall be taken up and continued in accordance with the new Act.
If a notice has been sent under subsection 25.3(2) of the former Act in respect of the investment before the day on which section 15 comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall, without delay after that day, send to the non-Canadian making the investment and to any person or entity from which the Canadian business or the entity referred to in paragraph 25.1(c) of the new Act is being acquired, a notice referred to in subsection 25.3(2) of the new Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed period for the purposes of subsection 25.3(6) of the new Act is the period beginning on the day on which the order under subsection 25.3(1) of the former Act is made and ending 45 days after that day.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
